content.text:
<user_query>
Re-check HIGH parity issues only after latest admin-role tightening. Scope unchanged (backend core/config, tier_guard, tier_resolver, deps, auth_supabase, routes/email; frontend App, tierResolver, SupabaseAuthContext, routeAccessConfig, ProtectedRoute, DashboardLayout, tiers, pricingTiers). Return HIGH only or NONE.
</user_query>
Reviewing scoped backend and frontend files for HIGH-severity parity issues after admin-role tightening.

## HIGH

**`DashboardLayout` user menu vs tightened super-admin gates:** The header dropdown still treats `user.role === 'admin'` the same as real super admins for the “Super Admin” item (`user?.role === 'admin' || user?.role === 'superadmin' || isPrivilegedUser(user)`), but `ProtectedRoute` `adminOnly` only accepts `superadmin` / `super_admin` plus master email (`ADMIN_ROLES` does not include `admin`), matching `get_super_admin` in `deps.py`. So after admin-role tightening, **client `admin` still gets a super-admin affordance that the scoped guards reject** — a HIGH parity / semantics mismatch in the files you listed.

---

Everything else in the scoped set lines up for **critical** parity: `tier_resolver` / `routeAccessConfig` / `WAITLIST_*` / `LaunchRoute` waitlist bypass (`tier === 'super_admin'`), `ProtectedRoute` admin check, and `has_admin_role` / `check_api_access` super_admin paths are consistent; `config.py` has no tier logic; `email.py` only uses `resolve_tier` / master check in the snippets seen; `pricingTiers` / `tiers` stay product-tier only.

**Note (not counted as a separate HIGH here):** The sidebar Admin block uses `isSA = isPrivilegedUser(user)` (master email only), while backend `resolve_tier` also promotes `superadmin` / `super_admin` roles — so **role-based super admins get backend/admin routes but not the same sidebar block as the master email**. That’s parity drift on **where** super-admin UI appears, not an extra access path.
